summary_en: WACC revised upward to ~10% (from ~8%); ROE target maintained at 12.0%+ by FY3/28; equity ratio to be managed within 70%; minimum payout ratio 40%+; three-year cumulative total payout ratio 50%+, dividend floor maintained.
title_en: Update on Management Focus on Capital Cost and Stock Price
translation_en_brief: ESPEC filed an update to its cost-of-capital and shareholder-value management framework on 26 May 2026. The company revised its weighted-average cost of capital upward to approximately 10% (from ~8%), reflecting rising rates and investor dialogue. The ROE target under its three-year plan PROGRESSIVE PLUS 2027 (FY3/25–27, now extended through FY3/28) remains 12.0%+ as a floor. Current-year ROE of 10.0% fell short of the initial 11.0% goal, prompting the company to strengthen financial discipline. Going forward, the company will manage equity ratio at or below 70% (previously emphasis on high capital buffers), leverage debt where appropriate for growth and value creation, and allocate cash under a three-year capital plan with a dividend payout floor of 40% and three-year cumulative total payout ratio of 50%+, with no dividend cuts during the plan period.
